metadata.title: PMDA Guideline on Bioanalytical Method Validation in Pharmaceutical Development
metadata.note: Japan-specific BMV guideline predating ICH M10. Contains Japan-specific numeric criteria and requirements distinct from the harmonized ICH M10 guideline extracted in output-v4.

rules.rule_text: A certificate of analysis or alternative statement providing lot number, content (purity), and storage conditions must accompany the reference standard.
rules.verbatim_quote: A certificate of analysis or an alternative statement that provides information on lot number, content (purity), and storage conditions should accompany the standard.
rules.exceptions: A certificate of analysis is not necessarily required for an internal standard.

rules.rule_text: The lack of analytical interference between the internal standard and the analyte must be demonstrated before use.
rules.verbatim_quote: the lack of analytical interference with the analyte should be demonstrated before use as the internal standard.

rules.rule_text: An analytical method validation must be performed when establishing a bioanalytical method for quantification in every facility.
rules.verbatim_quote: An analytical method validation should be performed when establishing a bioanalytical method for quantification in every facility.

rules.rule_text: A full validation must be performed when establishing a new bioanalytical method for quantification of an analyte/analytes, covering selectivity, LLOQ, calibration curve, accuracy, precision, matrix effect, carry-over, dilution integrity, and stability.
rules.verbatim_quote: A full validation should be performed when establishing a new bioanalytical method for quantification of an analyte/analytes. The objective of a full validation is to demonstrate the assay performance of the method, e.g. selectivity, lower limit of quantification (LLOQ), calibration curve, accuracy, precision, matrix effect, carry-over, dilution integrity, and stability.

rules.rule_text: A full validation must be performed for each species and matrix (plasma, serum, whole blood, or urine) to be analyzed.
rules.verbatim_quote: Generally, a full validation should be performed for each species and matrices (mainly plasma, serum, whole blood, or urine) to be analyzed.

rules.rule_text: A full validation is required when a new analyte (e.g., metabolite) is added to an existing validated method, or when implementing an analytical method from literature.
rules.verbatim_quote: A full validation should also be considered when a new analyte, such as a metabolite, is added to an existing, fully validated analytical method. A full validation is also required when implementing an analytical method from a literature.

rules.rule_text: The use of a surrogate matrix for rare matrices must be rigorously justified in the course of establishing the analytical method.
rules.verbatim_quote: the use of a surrogate matrix should be rigorously justified in the course of establishing the analytical method.
rules.exceptions: Surrogate matrix may be used when a sufficient amount of matrix cannot be obtained from sufficient number of sources for rare matrices (tissue, cerebrospinal fluid, bile).

rules.rule_text: Selectivity must be evaluated using blank samples obtained from at least 6 individual sources. The absence of interference with each analyte and its internal standard must be confirmed.
rules.verbatim_quote: Selectivity is evaluated using blank samples (matrix samples processed without addition of an analyte or internal standard) obtained from at least 6 individual sources. The absence of interference with each analyte and its internal standard should be confirmed.
rules.exceptions: In case of the matrix with limited availability, it may be acceptable to use matrix samples obtained from less than 6 sources.

rules.rule_text: Interfering component response must be no higher than 20% of the LLOQ response for the analyte and no higher than 5% of the internal standard response.
rules.verbatim_quote: a response attributable to interfering components is not higher than 20% of the response in the LLOQ for the analyte and also not higher than 5% of the internal standard.

rules.rule_text: The analyte response at the LLOQ must be at least 5 times the response of a blank sample. Mean accuracy and precision at the LLOQ must be within +/-20% deviation and not more than 20% CV respectively.
rules.verbatim_quote: The analyte response at the LLOQ should be at least 5 times the response of that in a blank sample. Mean accuracy and precision at the LLOQ should be within +/-20% deviation of the nominal (theoretical) concentration and not more than 20%, respectively.

rules.rule_text: A calibration curve must include a blank sample, a zero sample (blank spiked with internal standard), and at least 6 concentration levels of calibration standards including an LLOQ sample.
rules.verbatim_quote: A calibration curve should be generated with a blank sample, a zero sample (blank sample spiked with internal standard), and at least 6 concentration levels of calibration standards, including an LLOQ sample.

rules.rule_text: Blank and zero samples must not be included in the determination of the regression equation for the calibration curve.
rules.verbatim_quote: Blank and zero samples should not be included in the determination of the regression equation for the calibration curve.

rules.rule_text: Back-calculated concentrations of calibration standards must be within +/-20% deviation at the LLOQ or +/-15% at all other levels. At least 75% of standards (minimum 6 levels including LLOQ and highest) must meet criteria.
rules.verbatim_quote: The accuracy of back calculated concentrations of each calibration standard should be within +/-20% deviation of the theoretical concentration at the LLOQ, or +/-15% deviation at all the other levels. At least 75% of the calibration standards, with a minimum of 6 levels, including the LLOQ and the highest levels, should meet the above criteria.

rules.rule_text: QC samples for accuracy/precision validation must include a minimum of 4 concentrations (LLOQ, low, mid, high). Low-level must be within 3x LLOQ, mid around midpoint, high at least 75% of upper limit of calibration curve.
rules.verbatim_quote: QC samples with a minimum of 4 different concentrations (LLOQ and low-, mid-, and high-levels) within the calibration range are prepared. The low-level should be within 3 times the LLOQ, the mid-level is around the midpoint on the calibration curve, and the high-level should be at least 75% of the upper limit of the calibration curve.

rules.rule_text: Within-run accuracy and precision must be evaluated by at least 5 replicates at each concentration level. Between-run evaluation requires at least 3 analytical runs.
rules.verbatim_quote: Within-run accuracy and precision should be evaluated by at least 5 replicates at each concentration level in a single analytical run. Between-run accuracy and precision should be evaluated by the analysis in at least 3 analytical runs.

rules.rule_text: Mean accuracy must be within +/-15% of theoretical concentration (or +/-20% at LLOQ). Precision (CV) must not exceed 15% (or 20% at LLOQ).
rules.verbatim_quote: The mean accuracy at each concentration level should be within +/-15% deviation of the theoretical concentration, except at the LLOQ, where it should be within +/-20%. Precision of concentrations determined at each level should not exceed 15%, except at the LLOQ, where it should not exceed 20%.

rules.rule_text: Matrix effect must be assessed when using mass spectrometric methods by calculating the matrix factor (MF) using matrix from at least 6 different sources.
rules.verbatim_quote: Matrix effect should be assessed when using mass spectrometric methods. Matrix effect is evaluated by calculating the matrix factor (MF)... MF should be calculated using matrix from at least 6 different sources.
rules.exceptions: In case the matrix is of limited availability, it may be acceptable to use matrix obtained from less than 6 sources.

rules.rule_text: The precision of the matrix factor (IS-normalized) calculated from at least 6 sources must not exceed 15% CV.
rules.verbatim_quote: The precision of the MF calculated should not exceed 15%.

rules.rule_text: Carry-over must be evaluated by analyzing a blank sample following the highest concentration calibration standard. The response must not be greater than 20% of the analyte response at the LLOQ and not greater than 5% of the internal standard response.
rules.verbatim_quote: The carry-over should be evaluated by analyzing a blank sample following the highest concentration calibration standard. The response in the blank sample obtained after the highest concentration standard should not be greater than 20% of the analyte response at the LLOQ and also not greater than 5% of the response of internal standard.
rules.exceptions: If the criteria cannot be met, the impact of carry-over needs to be examined, and appropriate procedures should be taken to avoid any biases during the analysis of actual study samples.

rules.rule_text: Dilution integrity must be evaluated by at least 5 replicates per dilution factor. Mean accuracy and precision of diluted samples must be within +/-15% deviation and not more than 15% CV respectively.
rules.verbatim_quote: Dilution integrity should be evaluated by at least 5 replicates per dilution factor after diluting a sample with blank matrix to bring the analyte concentration within the calibration range... Mean accuracy and precision in the measurements of diluted samples should be within +/-15% deviation of the theoretical concentration and not more than 15%, respectively.

rules.rule_text: Analyte stability must be evaluated to ensure concentration is not affected through each step from sample collection to analysis, under conditions as close to actual circumstances as possible.
rules.verbatim_quote: Analyte stability should be evaluated to ensure that the concentration is not affected by the samples through each step of the process from the sample collection to the analysis. The stability of the samples should be assessed under conditions that are as close to the actual circumstances, e.g. sample storage and sample analysis as much as possible.

rules.rule_text: Validation studies must determine analyte stability after freeze and thaw cycles, after short-term storage (room temperature, on ice, or under refrigeration), and after long-term storage. Processed sample stability must also be considered.
rules.verbatim_quote: Validation studies should determine analyte stability after freeze and thaw cycles, after short-term (at room temperature, on ice, or under refrigeration) and long-term storage; stability in the processed samples should also be considered.

rules.rule_text: Stability experiments must be performed on samples stored for a time that is longer than the actual storage period.
rules.verbatim_quote: All stability experiments should be performed on samples that have been stored for a time that is longer than the actual storage period.

rules.rule_text: Stock and working solution stability must be evaluated using solutions near the highest and lowest concentration levels, with at least 3 replicates at each level.
rules.verbatim_quote: Stability of the analyte in the stock and working solutions is usually evaluated using solutions near the highest and lowest concentration levels. The evaluation is performed by at least 3 replicates at each concentration level.

rules.rule_text: Matrix stability must be evaluated using low- and high-level QC samples with at least 3 replicates per concentration level. Mean accuracy must be within +/-15% of theoretical concentration.
rules.verbatim_quote: Stability of the analyte in the studied matrix is evaluated using low- and high-level QC samples... Stability is evaluated by at least 3 replicates per concentration level with QC samples before and after storage. The mean accuracy in the measurements at each level should be within +/-15% deviation of the theoretical concentration, in principle.
rules.exceptions: If other criteria are more appropriate for the evaluation of specific analyte, they could be used.

rules.rule_text: Acceptance criteria used in partial validation must be the same as those employed in the full validation in principle.
rules.verbatim_quote: Acceptance criteria used in partial validation should be the same as those employed in the full validation in principle.

rules.rule_text: Cross validation among two or more laboratories requires mean accuracy of QC samples (low-, mid-, and high-levels) at each level to be within +/-20% of theoretical concentration, evaluated by at least 3 replicates.
rules.verbatim_quote: In the cross validation among two or more laboratories within a study, the mean accuracy of QC samples (low-, mid-, and high-levels) evaluated by at least 3 replicates at each level, should be within +/-20% deviation of the theoretical concentration, considering intra- and inter-laboratories precision.

rules.rule_text: When using a set of study samples for cross validation, assay variability must be within +/-20% for at least two-thirds of the samples.
rules.verbatim_quote: When using a set of study samples, the assay variability should be within +/-20% for at least two-thirds of the samples.

rules.rule_text: Study samples must be analyzed using a fully validated analytical method and handled under conditions validated for adequate stability, within the confirmed stability period.
rules.verbatim_quote: Analysis of study samples should be carried out using a fully validated analytical method In the analysis, study samples should be handled under conditions that are validated for adequate stability, and analyzed within a confirmed stability period.

rules.rule_text: Each analytical run of study samples must include a blank sample, a zero sample, calibration standards at a minimum of 6 concentration levels, and QC samples.
rules.verbatim_quote: study samples should be handled under conditions that are validated for adequate stability, and analyzed within a confirmed stability period, along with a blank sample, a zero sample, calibration standards at a minimum of 6 concentration levels, and QC samples.

rules.rule_text: For studies using PK data as a primary endpoint, reproducibility of the analytical method must be confirmed by performing incurred sample reanalysis (ISR) for each representative study per matrix.
rules.verbatim_quote: In studies that serve pharmacokinetic data as a primary endpoint, reproducibility of the analytical method should be confirmed for each representative study per matrix by performing incurred sample reanalysis.

rules.rule_text: Study sample calibration standard back-calculated concentrations must be within +/-20% at LLOQ or +/-15% at other levels. At least 75% of standards (minimum 6 levels) must meet criteria.
rules.verbatim_quote: The accuracy of back calculated concentrations of calibration standards at each level should be within +/-20% deviation of the theoretical concentration at the LLOQ, or +/-15% deviation at all other levels. At least 75% of the calibration standards, with a minimum of 6 levels, should meet the above criteria.
rules.exceptions: In case the calibration standard at the LLOQ or the highest level did not meet the criteria in study sample analysis, the next lowest/highest-level calibration standard may be used as the LLOQ or the upper limit of the calibration curve.

rules.rule_text: Each analytical run must include QC samples at minimum 3 concentration levels. Analysis requires 2 QC samples at each level or at least 5% of total study samples, whichever is greater.
rules.verbatim_quote: QC samples with a minimum of 3 different concentration levels (low-, mid-, and high-levels) within the calibration range are analyzed in each analytical run... The analysis requires 2 QC samples at each QC level or at least 5% of the total number of study samples in the analytical run, whichever is the greater.

rules.rule_text: QC sample accuracy must be within +/-15% of theoretical concentrations. At least two-thirds of QC samples and at least 50% at each concentration level must meet criteria.
rules.verbatim_quote: The accuracy in the measurements of QC samples should be within +/-15% deviation of the theoretical concentrations. At least two-thirds of the QC samples and at least 50% at each concentration level should meet the criteria.

rules.rule_text: ISR must be conducted for: toxicokinetic studies for each different species; clinical PK studies for healthy volunteers; patients with renal/hepatic impairment; and bioequivalence studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: ISR should be conducted in the following situations: toxicokinetic studies for each different species; clinical studies representative pharmacokinetic studies for healthy volunteers and patients with renal/hepatic impairment, as well as in bioequivalence studies.
rules.exceptions: For non-clinical studies, ISR may be performed with samples obtained in a independent non-GLP study, if the study design is similar to the relevant toxicokintics study.

rules.rule_text: ISR samples must include those near maximum blood concentration (Cmax) and the elimination phase. Approximately 10% of samples must be reanalyzed when total samples are less than 1000, and approximately 5% when exceeding 1000 samples.
rules.verbatim_quote: ISR should be performed with samples from as many subjects or animals as possible and should usually include those of near the maximum blood concentration (Cmax) and the elimination phase... approximately 10% of the samples should be reanalyzed in cases where the total number of study samples is less than 1000 and approximately 5% of the number of samples exceeding 1000 samples.

rules.rule_text: ISR assay variability must be within +/-20% for at least two-thirds of the samples analyzed. If criteria are not met, cause investigation must be conducted and remedial measures taken.
rules.verbatim_quote: The assay variability should be within +/-20% for at least two-thirds of the samples analyzed in ISR. In case the ISR data failed to meet the above criteria, cause investigation should be conducted for the analytical method and necessary measures should be taken by considering the potential impact on study sample analysis.

rules.rule_text: Original ISR data must never be discarded or replaced with the reanalysis data even if assay variability exceeds +/-20% for a specific sample.
rules.verbatim_quote: The original data should never be discarded or replaced with the reanalysis data even if the assay variability exceeds +/-20% in a specific sample.

rules.rule_text: In bioequivalence studies, reanalysis of study samples for PK reasons only is not acceptable. Reanalysis is only acceptable when initial analysis yields unexpected or anomalous results that may affect patient safety.
rules.verbatim_quote: In bioequivalence studies, it is not acceptable to reanalyze study samples only because the initial data were pharmacokinetically questionable in order to replace the concentration data. However, reanalysis of specific study samples are acceptable when, for instance, the initial analysis yielded an unexpected or anomalous result that may affect the patient safety in a clinical trial.
rules.exceptions: Reanalysis acceptable when calibration curve or QC samples failed validity criteria; concentration above upper limit; analyte detected in pre-dose/placebo samples; improper sample processing or equipment malfunction; defective chromatogram; causal investigation of abnormal values.

rules.rule_text: When reanalysis is performed, the analytical report must document: information on reanalyzed samples, reason for reanalysis, data from initial analysis, data from reanalysis, final accepted values, and reason and method of selection.
rules.verbatim_quote: In any case, when reanalysis is performed, the analytical report should provide information of the reanalyzed samples; the reason for reanalysis; the data obtained in the initial analysis, if any; the data obtained in the reanalysis; and the final accepted values and the reason and method of selection.

rules.rule_text: Procedures for chromatogram integration and re-integration must be predefined in the protocol or SOP. When re-integration is performed, reasons and chromatograms before and after must be recorded and kept.
rules.verbatim_quote: Procedures for chromatogram integration and re-integration should be predefined in the protocol or SOP. In case chromatogram re-integration is performed, the reason for re-integration should be recorded and the chromatograms obtained both before and after re-integration should be kept for future reference.

rules.rule_text: All relevant records and raw data must be kept, including those from rejected analytical runs. This encompasses records of reference materials, blank matrices, samples, instruments, deviations, communications, and raw data including chromatograms.
rules.verbatim_quote: All relevant records and raw data should be kept, including those obtained in rejected analytical runs, specifically record of reference materials and blank matrices (receipt/release, use, storage), record of samples (receipt/release, preparation, and storage), record of analyses, record of instrument (calibration and settings), record of deviations, record of communications, and raw data such as analytical data and chromatograms.
